_meta.cycle_disposition_reason: Weekly bundle contains 5 new developments across trade policy, energy shock, and regulatory domains with multiple T1 sources.

lead_signal.headline: ECB President Lagarde warns Strait of Hormuz closure represents largest oil supply disruption in history amid relentless shock cascade
lead_signal.body: ECB characterizes period as relentless shock cascade including pandemic, land war, energy crisis, sweeping tariffs since 1930s, and now Hormuz closure. This energy shock compounds April 2 Liberation Day tariff package creating dual supply-side shocks. ECB baseline assumes contained conflict but adverse scenarios show prolonged disruption would significantly reduce growth and elevate inflation beyond baseline.

ECB baseline assumes contained conflict but adverse scenarios show prolonged disruption would significantly reduce growth and elevate inflation beyond baseline.

announcements.headline: ECB President Lagarde warns Strait of Hormuz closure represents largest oil supply disruption in history
announcements.asymmetric: Energy shock compounds tariff cascade creating dual supply shocks that lock stagflation risk; ECB baseline assumes contained conflict but adverse scenarios show prolonged disruption would significantly reduce growth

announcements.headline: ECB holds rates amid energy shock, projects 2026 inflation at 2.6% on Middle East war impact
announcements.asymmetric: Headline inflation projected 2.6% for 2026 up from December forecast driven by energy price increases; ECB baseline assumes contained conflict but adverse scenarios show prolonged disruption would significantly reduce growth

announcements.headline: Federal Reserve expected to hold rates April 28-29 amid tariff and energy uncertainty
announcements.asymmetric: March FOMC minutes noted AI disruptions and Middle East conflict raised questions about macroeconomic outlook; G-7 central banks coordinating holds this week watching energy costs and inflation risks

announcements.headline: BIS warns crypto exchanges operate as shadow banks without prudential safeguards citing systemic risk
announcements.asymmetric: BIS characterizes major crypto exchanges as shadow crypto financial system engaging in risk transformation that exposes users to credit liquidity and maturity risks; regulatory perimeter expansion likely over multi-year horizon

announcements.headline: ECB enhances EUREP liquidity facility for non-euro central banks citing volatile global environment
announcements.asymmetric: ECB introduces standing access for all central banks unless excluded for AML sanctions reasons reflecting more uncertain and potentially more volatile global environment with frequent financial disruptions

module_9.title: Trade and Tariff Policy
module_9.digest_note: Tariff rung 3 stable but USTR signals enforcement; national ratifications pending could trigger new retaliators
module_9.friction_analysis: EU-US Turnberry tariff deal locks in 15% rate on EU exports reducing uncertainty but eroding EU competitiveness as tariffs on rivals like China converge to similar levels. EU steel and aluminum face sustained 25% and 10% US Section 232 tariffs creating high costs for EU auto machinery construction exporters. Part of overlapping regimes with no resolution in sight despite negotiations.
module_9.tariff_escalation_rung: Tariff escalation rung remains at 3 with enacted tariffs and active EU retaliation. USTR 2026 Trade Policy Agenda doubles down on America First tariffs signaling sustained high-tariff regime without de-escalation. Risk of escalation to rung 4 via USMCA review failure with likelihood 0.5 impact 0.8.

macro_health_composite.note: Dual supply shocks from tariffs and energy create stagflationary environment that locks in growth headwinds beyond typical cyclical downturn

USTR agenda enforcement plus EM exporter tariff convergence risks sovereign stress to conflict financing
